expert_rss.title: Distinctive Hallmarks: Investigations into Online Subcultures of Nihilistic Violence
expert_rss.summary: Content warning: This Insight contains language describing self-harm and suicide. In recent months, a growing concern over online subcultures of nihilistic violence, or Nihilistic Violent Extremism (NVE), has influenced how governments are tackling extremist threats. Canada, for instance, designated the decentralised transnational NVE network known as “764” as a terrorist entity in December 2025.

expert_rss.title: Moody Air Force Base Installs Refueling Probe, Improves Efficiency
expert_rss.summary: Airmen assigned to Moody Air Force Base, Ga., installed the base's first air refueling probe on an A-10C Thunderbolt II subsonic attack aircraft, enhancing its ability to support combat search and rescue and close-air support operations.

expert_rss.title: Hegseth Praises U.S. Military Efforts to Protect Nigerian Christians From ISIS
expert_rss.summary: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised recent efforts the U.S. military has made to counter the Islamic State and its ongoing murder of Christians in Nigeria.

expert_rss.title: Sword 26: Cyber Defenders Train in Estonia, Secure Critical Infrastructure
expert_rss.summary: U.S. soldiers and National Guard cyber professionals trained with members of the Estonian Defense Forces to defend the networks that modern societies rely on daily during a Sword 26 simulated exercise.

expert_rss.title: Coast Guard Commissions 62nd Fast Response Cutter Honoring 9/11 Hero
expert_rss.summary: The Coast Guard commissioned its newest fast response cutter, the Vincent Danz, for official entry into its service fleet during a ceremony in New York City.

expert_rss.title: Pennsylvania Guard Shapes Army's Extended Basic Leader Course
expert_rss.summary: National Guardsmen assigned to Fort Indiantown Gap, Pa., are leading the validation effort for the Army's expanded basic leader course, refining the curriculum before it is implemented forcewide later this year.

expert_rss.title: District of Columbia National Guard Unveils Freedom 250 Patch

expert_rss.summary: The service’s shore food service transformation initiative will begin at two installations and focus on revamping food quality and providing more options.

expert_rss.title: US munitions depleted by Iran war will take years to restore, analysis finds
expert_rss.summary: Replenishing stockpiles of critical weapons systems could take until 2030 or 2031, creating a "window of vulnerability," a new analysis found.

expert_rss.title: How US Army combat medics are preparing for an Indo-Pacific fight
expert_rss.summary: Distance, contested air, communication disruptions, weather and limited evacuation platforms could greatly impact timely access to medical care.

expert_rss.title: US Army integrates veterinarians into human combat care
expert_rss.summary: The Army wants to better utilize veterinarians in combat medicine to prepare for the possibility of large-scale operations in the Indo-Pacific.

expert_rss.title: US Army receives first lightweight Javelin launchers
expert_rss.summary: The U.S. Army has received its first batch of new, lighter Javelin launchers, defense contractor RTX announced in a statement Tuesday.

wikipedia.notes: An individual with known history of violence and mental issues stabbed and injured a Muslim and two Jewish men in a series of attacks. The Metropolitan Police declared the incident an act of terrorism.

At 8:55 p.m. local time, a suicide bomber and multiple fighters detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a security post, causing the post to collapse, destroying nearby vehicles and severely damaging nearby civilian areas.
Shortly after 8:00 a.m. PKT, an explosives-laden vehicle struck one of the carriages of a shuttle train near Chaman Phatak as the train was travelling through Quetta.
